_source: Fair Housing Act, Landlord-Tenant law, IREM CPM standards, Property management best practices
_note: Long-tail property management knowledge. Fair Housing compliance, tenant screening, lease agreements, maintenance scheduling, eviction procedures, accounting.

protected_classes.federal: Race, Color, Religion, National origin, Sex (including sexual orientation and gender identity), Familial status (children under 18), Disability
protected_classes.state_local: Many states add: Source of income (Section 8), Age, Marital status, Sexual orientation (if not covered by federal sex), Gender identity; check state/local laws

prohibited_actions.refuse_to_rent: Cannot refuse based on protected class; applies to advertising, showing, application, approval
prohibited_actions.different_terms: Cannot charge different rent, deposits, fees, or have different rules based on protected class
prohibited_actions.discriminatory_advertising: Cannot state preference or limitation based on protected class; 'No children', 'Adults only', 'Christian community' all illegal
prohibited_actions.steering: Cannot direct people to specific units/buildings based on protected class; must show all available units that meet stated criteria
prohibited_actions.harassment: Cannot harass based on protected class; includes sexual harassment; hostile environment
prohibited_actions.retaliation: Cannot retaliate against tenants who file fair housing complaint or exercise fair housing rights

reasonable_accommodation_modification.accommodation: Policy/procedure change for disabled person; emotional support animals (not pets, no pet rent/deposit, but damage charges allowed), assigned parking near entrance, rent payment date change if disability affects income timing
reasonable_accommodation_modification.modification: Physical alteration for disabled person; grab bars, wheelchair ramps, lowered counters; tenant pays for modification; landlord can require restoration if not universally beneficial
reasonable_accommodation_modification.process: Tenant requests, provides documentation of disability and necessity; interactive process to find solution; cannot charge application/processing fee for request
reasonable_accommodation_modification.denial: Can only deny if undue financial/administrative burden or fundamental alteration of operations; must engage in good faith interactive process first

exemptions.owner_occupied_1_4_units: If owner lives in one unit of 1-4 unit building, exempt from Fair Housing Act (not state laws though); still cannot advertise discriminatorily
exemptions.single_family_rental: Exempt if owner doesn't own >3 single-family homes, no broker used, and no discriminatory advertising; limited exemption
exemptions.religious_organizations: Can give preference to members if nonprofit and not commercial
exemptions.senior_housing: 62+ or 80% 55+ can exclude families with children; must meet HUD certification requirements

penalties.first_violation: Up to $21,039 civil penalty (2024 amount, adjusts annually)
penalties.damages: Actual damages to victim, emotional distress, punitive damages (no cap), attorney fees; can exceed $100,000 easily
penalties.risk_management: Fair housing training for all staff; standardized screening criteria applied to all; document everything; never mention protected class

application_process.application_fee: Can charge actual cost of screening (credit check, background check); typically $30-75; cannot profit; some states cap amount or prohibit
application_process.required_disclosure: Must disclose screening criteria before application; rejection criteria (credit score, income, criminal history, rental history); consistency critical
application_process.written_criteria: Objective, verifiable criteria; minimum credit score (600, 620, etc.), income 2.5-3× rent, no evictions last 5-7 years, references; apply equally to all

credit_check.credit_score: FICO typical; 620+ good, 580-619 marginal, <580 high risk; can require co-signer if below threshold
credit_check.credit_history: Payment history, collections, judgments, bankruptcies; bankruptcy cannot be sole reason for denial but can be considered with other factors
credit_check.adverse_action: If deny based on credit report, must provide adverse action notice with credit bureau info, score, factors; FCRA requirement

criminal_background.hud_guidance: Blanket ban on anyone with criminal history is discriminatory (disparate impact); must consider nature, severity, recency of crime; individualized assessment
criminal_background.violent_sexual_crimes: Can deny for violent crimes, sexual offenses; especially recent (last 5-7 years); safety of other residents justifies
criminal_background.drug_related: Can deny for drug manufacturing/distribution; simple possession less clear; consider timeframe and rehabilitation
criminal_background.arrest_vs_conviction: Cannot use arrests without conviction; presumption of innocence; can use convictions
criminal_background.look_back_period: 7 years typical; some states limit how far back can look; consider nature of crime and time elapsed

income_verification.income_requirement: 2.5-3× monthly rent in gross income standard; some use 30-40% of gross income for rent; must be consistent policy
income_verification.acceptable_documentation: Paystubs (2-3 recent), offer letter, tax returns (self-employed, commission), bank statements, pension/SS award letters, Section 8 voucher
income_verification.self_employed: 2 years tax returns; average income; CPA letter; consider trends (increasing vs decreasing)
income_verification.co_signers: If applicant doesn't meet income, co-signer can guarantee; co-signer must meet income requirement (3-5× rent sometimes); separate application/screening

rental_history.landlord_verification: Contact prior landlords (not current - may have incentive to lie to get rid of problem tenant); verify tenancy dates, rent paid on time, lease violations, damage, would rent again
rental_history.eviction_search: Court records; shows evictions filed (even if dismissed); automatic denial for eviction last 5-7 years common; nuance: eviction filed doesn't mean tenant at fault
rental_history.employment_verification: Call employer (or letter from HR); verify position, hire date, salary; some employers only confirm dates/title (policy)
rental_history.references: Personal references low value (applicant chooses); professional references better; prior landlord reference most valuable

decision_making.consistent_criteria: CRITICAL: Apply same standards to all applicants; document reason for every denial; avoid subjective judgments ('seems flaky')
decision_making.first_qualified_applicant: Best practice: rent to first applicant who meets objective criteria; prevents discrimination claims; timestamp applications
decision_making.denial_notice: Must provide written notice with specific reason; adverse action notice if credit/background check used; allow opportunity to dispute inaccuracy
decision_making.holding_deposits: Some allow to hold unit while processing; typically applied to security deposit or refunded; rules vary by state; get agreement in writing

essential_terms.parties: Names of all adults; landlord/agent contact info; property address with unit number
essential_terms.term: Start and end date (fixed-term), or month-to-month; auto-renewal clause (converts to month-to-month or renews for term)
essential_terms.rent: Amount, due date, grace period (if any), late fee amount and when assessed, acceptable payment methods
essential_terms.security_deposit: Amount, conditions for deductions, return timeline (state law typically 14-30 days), interest if required by state
essential_terms.utilities: Who pays what (water, sewer, electric, gas, trash, internet); if tenant-paid, must be clearly stated
essential_terms.occupancy_limits: Number of occupants; names of all residents; guest policy
essential_terms.use_restrictions: Residential use only; no illegal activity; quiet enjoyment; no commercial use without permission

common_clauses.maintenance_responsibilities: Landlord: structure, roof, plumbing, electrical, HVAC, appliances; Tenant: cleanliness, minor repairs, changing filters, yard care (if applicable), damage caused by tenant
common_clauses.entry_notice: Landlord entry with 24-48 hour notice (varies by state) except emergency; specify business hours; can enter without notice if emergency (fire, flood, gas leak)
common_clauses.pet_policy: Pets allowed/prohibited; pet deposit/rent; restrictions (size, breed, number); emotional support animals exempt from pet fees (reasonable accommodation)
common_clauses.smoking_policy: Smoke-free property or designated areas; many states/cities allow landlords to prohibit smoking
common_clauses.assignment_subletting: Typically prohibited without landlord consent; or allowed with consent not to be unreasonably withheld
common_clauses.lead_paint_disclosure: Required for pre-1978 properties; provide EPA pamphlet; disclosure form; 10-day inspection period; $11,000+ penalty for non-compliance
common_clauses.mold_disclosure: Some states require disclosure of past mold issues; prevention responsibilities
common_clauses.bed_bugs: Some jurisdictions require disclosure; prevention and treatment responsibilities; who pays for extermination

security_deposits.maximum_limits: Varies by state; 1-2 months' rent typical; some states no limit; check local law
security_deposits.allowable_deductions: Unpaid rent, damage beyond normal wear and tear, cleaning if left excessively dirty, lease break fees if specified in lease
security_deposits.normal_wear_and_tear: Expected deterioration from everyday use; minor carpet wear, nail holes, paint fading; NOT damage; cannot charge for normal wear and tear
security_deposits.itemized_deductions: Must provide itemized list with receipts/invoices; cannot estimate; actual costs only; if fail to provide itemization within statutory time, may forfeit deposit and owe penalties
security_deposits.return_timeline: 14-60 days depending on state; California 21 days, Florida 15-30 days; penalties for late return (double or triple deposit common)
security_deposits.interest: Some states require interest on deposits (NJ, IL, NH, NY); rate varies; annual or at lease end
security_deposits.separate_account: Some states require separate account or posting of bond; cannot commingle with operating funds

lease_violations.notice_to_cure: Give tenant notice of violation and time to cure (3-10 days typical); 'Pay or Quit' (rent), 'Cure or Quit' (lease violation)
lease_violations.non_curable: Some violations can't be cured: illegal activity, serious damage, repeated violations; can proceed directly to eviction
lease_violations.documentation: Photograph violations, written notices (certified mail or hand-delivered with proof), keep copies, document communications
lease_violations.escalation: First violation: warning/cure notice; second: cure notice with eviction threat; third: eviction filing; except serious violations (immediate eviction)

preventive_maintenance_schedule.monthly: HVAC filter change (or educate tenants to do it), test GFCI outlets, check fire extinguishers, inspect common areas, landscape maintenance
preventive_maintenance_schedule.quarterly: HVAC professional service (coil cleaning, refrigerant check, belt inspection), gutter cleaning, pest control treatment, irrigation system check
preventive_maintenance_schedule.semi_annual: Water heater flush/anode rod check, dryer vent cleaning, window/door seal inspection, exterior paint touch-up, roof inspection
preventive_maintenance_schedule.annual: Appliance inspection/service, smoke/CO detector replacement (10-year lifespan), plumbing inspection, electrical panel inspection, tree trimming, parking lot seal coating, pool inspection/certification
preventive_maintenance_schedule.benefits: Prevents emergency repairs (cheaper to prevent than fix), extends asset life, maintains tenant satisfaction, fewer late-night calls

emergency_vs_routine.emergency: 24-hour response required; health/safety hazard; fire, flood, gas leak, no heat in winter, no AC in extreme heat (>100°F), sewage backup, major water leak, electrical hazard, lockout (after hours), broken window/door (security)
emergency_vs_routine.urgent: 24-48 hour response; impacts habitability; minor leak, clogged drain, appliance failure (fridge, stove), broken lock, no hot water, pest infestation
emergency_vs_routine.routine: 5-7 day response; inconvenience but not habitability issue; dripping faucet, squeaky door, light fixture out, cosmetic damage, outlet not working
emergency_vs_routine.tenant_caused: Tenant pays for damage they caused; clogged toilet (misuse), broken window (accident/negligence), lost keys, pest infestation (uncleanliness)

work_order_management.intake: Online portal, phone, email; log immediately with date/time, description, tenant name/unit, priority
work_order_management.assignment: Assign to in-house or vendor; track status (open, assigned, in progress, completed); communicate timeline to tenant
work_order_management.access_coordination: Schedule entry with tenant (24-48 hr notice unless emergency); confirm appointment; provide window (9-12, 1-4)
work_order_management.completion: Tenant sign-off or photo documentation; close work order; invoice processing; follow-up satisfaction call
work_order_management.software: AppFolio, Buildium, Propertyware; track all work orders; reporting (response time, cost per unit, vendor performance)

vendor_management.qualified_vendors: Licensed, insured (general liability $1M+, workers comp), bonded; W-9 for tax purposes; verify license/insurance annually
vendor_management.scope_of_work: Written scope for all projects >$500; get 3 bids for major work (>$5,000); include timeline, materials, cost breakdown
vendor_management.service_agreements: HVAC, plumbing, electrical, pest control, landscaping; preferred vendor status; pre-negotiated rates; priority response
vendor_management.performance_tracking: Response time, quality of work, cost, tenant feedback; review quarterly; replace poor performers
vendor_management.1099_reporting: Issue 1099-NEC if paid >$600/year; track payments; due January 31; penalties for non-compliance

capital_improvements.capex_vs_repair: Repair = returns to previous condition, expense immediately; Capital improvement = extends life, adds value, depreciate over 27.5 years (residential)
capital_improvements.common_capex: Roof replacement (20-30 year life), HVAC replacement (15-20 years), water heater (10-15 years), appliances (10-15 years), flooring, exterior paint, parking lot
capital_improvements.reserve_study: Estimate future capital needs; set aside reserves monthly; typical 5-10% of rent for reserves; prevents cash flow crunch
capital_improvements.improvement_planning: Budget annual capex; prioritize by necessity (roof leak) vs value-add (granite counters); coordinate with vacancy (floor/paint during turnover)

grounds_for_eviction.non_payment: Most common; rent unpaid by end of grace period; send Pay or Quit notice (3-5 days typical); if not paid, file eviction
grounds_for_eviction.lease_violation: Unauthorized occupant, pet, illegal activity, property damage, nuisance; send Cure or Quit notice (typically 10-30 days); if not cured, file eviction
grounds_for_eviction.holdover_tenancy: Lease ended, tenant didn't vacate; send notice to vacate (30-60 days if month-to-month, none needed if fixed-term expired); file eviction if stays
grounds_for_eviction.no_fault: End of lease term, owner move-in, sale, major renovation; notice required (30-60 days typically, 90-120 in some cities); just cause eviction laws limit this in some jurisdictions

eviction_process.notice: Proper notice required (Pay or Quit, Cure or Quit, Notice to Vacate); specific form requirements by state; hand-delivered or certified mail; wait period (3-30 days)
eviction_process.filing: File eviction complaint with court; pay filing fee ($100-300); serve tenant with summons and complaint (process server or sheriff)
eviction_process.court_hearing: Hearing within 10-30 days (varies); bring lease, notices, ledger, evidence; tenant can contest; judge issues judgment
eviction_process.writ_of_possession: If landlord wins, court issues writ; sheriff enforces; posts notice to vacate (24-72 hours); if tenant doesn't leave, sheriff removes tenant and belongings
eviction_process.lockout: Only sheriff can physically remove tenant; self-help eviction (changing locks, removing belongings without court order) is illegal and subjects landlord to liability

cash_for_keys.concept: Pay tenant to leave voluntarily; faster and cheaper than eviction ($200-2000 vs $1000-5000 and 1-3 months)
cash_for_keys.negotiation: Offer lump sum; tenant surrenders possession and signs release; no eviction on record (better for tenant); immediate possession (better for landlord)
cash_for_keys.agreement: Written agreement; move-out date, payment amount, release of claims, surrender of keys; pay at key handover; walk-through to verify vacant
cash_for_keys.when_to_use: Cooperative tenant in financial hardship, faster than eviction, avoid court costs and lost rent, avoid eviction moratorium delays

abandoned_property.determining_abandonment: Rent unpaid >15 days, no response to notices, utilities off, belongings mostly gone, neighbors report tenant moved; state law defines criteria
abandoned_property.notice_requirements: Post notice on door or send certified mail; state intent to consider property abandoned; deadline to claim belongings (10-30 days)
abandoned_property.disposal: After notice period, can dispose of belongings; some states require storage for period; document with photos; sell valuable items or donate; trash the rest
abandoned_property.vehicle_abandonment: Separate process; title search, notice to owner/lienholder, tow if not claimed; check DMV procedure

rent_collection.payment_methods: ACH/electronic (cheapest, fastest, encourage with discount), check, money order, cash (require receipt, count with tenant present, deposit same day), credit card (high fees 2-3%, pass to tenant or absorb)
rent_collection.grace_period: Rent due 1st, grace period to 3rd-5th typical; late fee after grace period; late fee $25-100 or 5-10% of rent; must be reasonable (some states cap)
rent_collection.partial_payments: Policy decision; accepting partial can restart notice period (check state law); written agreement if accepting payment plan
rent_collection.non_payment_escalation: Day 1: rent due; Day 6: late fee assessed, late notice; Day 10: Pay or Quit notice; Day 15: file eviction if unpaid; consistency crucial

accounting.chart_of_accounts: Income: rental income, late fees, pet rent, other income; Expenses: mortgage, property tax, insurance, repairs, maintenance, management fees, utilities, advertising, legal, bad debt
accounting.operating_account: Separate account for each property (or all properties); receives rent, pays expenses; never commingle with personal funds (pierces corporate veil, complicates taxes)
accounting.reserve_account: Separate savings for security deposits (if required) and capital reserves; don't touch for operating expenses
accounting.bank_reconciliation: Monthly; reconcile bank statement to accounting software; catch errors, fraud, duplicate charges; tenant ledgers should match bank deposits

financial_reporting.profit_loss_statement: Monthly and annual; income minus expenses = net operating income (NOI); owner's report; track trends
financial_reporting.balance_sheet: Assets (cash, accounts receivable, property value), Liabilities (mortgage, accounts payable, security deposits held), Equity
financial_reporting.cash_flow_statement: Sources and uses of cash; operating activities, investing, financing; important for budgeting and liquidity
financial_reporting.budget_vs_actual: Annual budget; compare actual to budget monthly; variance analysis; adjust operations or budget as needed

tax_considerations.rental_income: All rent and fees are taxable income; report on Schedule E
tax_considerations.deductible_expenses: Mortgage interest, property tax, insurance, repairs, maintenance, management fees, utilities (if landlord-paid), advertising, legal, travel (to property), depreciation
tax_considerations.depreciation: 27.5 year straight-line for residential; land not depreciable; improvement vs repair (capitalize vs expense)
tax_considerations.1099_requirements: Issue 1099-NEC to vendors paid >$600; corporations exempt (except attorneys); due to recipient by Jan 31, IRS by Mar 31
tax_considerations.security_deposits: Not income when received; becomes income if kept for damages/unpaid rent; refund is not expense (just returning tenant money)

key_performance_indicators.occupancy_rate: Occupied units / Total units; 95%+ good; <90% concerning; vacancy cost is rent lost plus turnover cost
key_performance_indicators.collection_rate: Rent collected / Rent charged; 98%+ excellent; includes evictions and bad debt; write off uncollectible after eviction
key_performance_indicators.turnover_rate: Move-outs / Total units; 30-50% annual typical; lower is better; cost to turn unit $1,000-3,000 (paint, clean, repairs, vacancy)
key_performance_indicators.maintenance_cost_per_unit: $500-1,500/unit/year average; track trends; spikes indicate deferred maintenance or mismanagement
key_performance_indicators.net_operating_income: Rental income - Operating expenses (excluding mortgage); used to calculate cap rate; cash flow = NOI - debt service

communication.response_time: Acknowledge within 24 hours; emergency = immediate; non-emergency = respond within 1 business day; update on progress every 2-3 days
communication.channels: Tenant portal (preferred, documented), email, phone, text; use portal for documentation trail
communication.documentation: Log all communications; date, time, method, summary; CYA for disputes; email confirmation of verbal conversations
communication.professionalism: Business relationship, not personal; friendly but not friends; boundaries important

lease_renewals.timing: Contact tenant 90 days before lease end; offer renewal or give non-renewal notice per lease terms
lease_renewals.rent_increases: Market analysis; 3-5% annual typical; larger increases risk vacancy; give 60-90 day notice; some jurisdictions have rent control limits
lease_renewals.renewal_incentive: Tenant retention cheaper than turnover; consider keeping rent same if good tenant, market soft, or recent improvements
lease_renewals.non_renewal: If not renewing tenant, give required notice (30-60 days typical, 90-120 in some cities); must have legitimate business reason in just-cause jurisdictions; be prepared for tenant to refuse to leave (eviction)

conflict_resolution.tenant_complaints: Listen, acknowledge, empathize, solve; most issues are miscommunication or unmet expectations; address promptly
conflict_resolution.neighbor_disputes: Mediate if both your tenants; document noise complaints; warn violator; enforce lease quiet enjoyment clause; evict if necessary
conflict_resolution.reasonable_accommodation_requests: Take seriously; engage in interactive process; verify disability and need; grant unless undue burden; document
conflict_resolution.rent_withholding: Some states allow if landlord doesn't make required repairs; establish repair timeline immediately; document all actions; consult attorney if tenant withholds rent

move_out_process.notice_to_vacate: Tenant must give 30-60 days notice (per lease); earlier notice doesn't relieve rent obligation unless lease allows
move_out_process.move_out_inspection: Schedule walk-through with tenant; document condition with photos/video; provide tenant with inspection checklist
move_out_process.cleaning_standards: Broom-clean typical; professional cleaning not required unless specified in lease; stove, fridge, bathrooms cleaned
move_out_process.forwarding_address: Required for security deposit return; if tenant doesn't provide, send to last known address (the rental unit) certified mail
move_out_process.turnover_timeline: Clean 1-2 days, paint 1-2 days, repairs 2-5 days, flooring/major work 1-2 weeks; 7-14 day turnover typical; market during turnover to minimize vacancy
